config.rules_description.other:
Match rules determine which browser opens a given URL automatically.

Rules can match by site (e.g. www.example.com), domain (e.g. example.com), or regular expression.

For now, rules are managed by editing the configuration file directly. A visual editor will be added in a future version.

config.make_default_label.other:
In order to Linkquisition to function as a browser-picker
it has to be set as the default browser:

config.scan_description.other:
The browsers should be scanned and stored in a configuration file for
faster startup and for enabling custom configuration.

The scan should be safe to execute at any time: only newly detected
browsers are added and the ones no longer present in the system are
removed.

Any existing rules, ordering or customization shouldn't be affected.

picker.keyboard_guide.other: Press 'ENTER' to pick first, 'ESC' to quit, '{{.CopyShortcut}}' to copy URL to clipboard

config.language_restart_note.other: Language change takes effect on next launch.

config.hide_keyboard_guide.other: Hide keyboard shortcut guide in picker

about.description.other: A fast, configurable browser-picker for Linux and macOS.

config.plugins_restart_note.other: Plugin changes take effect on next URL open.

config.browsers_empty.other:
No browsers configured yet.

Click the button below to scan your system for installed browsers. This is required for Linkquisition to work as a browser picker.

config.browsers_delete_confirm.other: Are you sure you want to delete "{{.Name}}"?

config.no_browsers_warning.other: ⚠ No browsers configured. Go to the Browsers tab to scan for installed browsers.

config.rules_no_browsers.other: No browsers configured. Add browsers first in the Browsers tab.

config.rules_empty.other:
No match rules configured yet.

Rules determine which browser opens a URL automatically. Use the "+ Add rule" button next to a browser name to create one.

config.macos_picker_note.other: Note: The browser picker window is not available while this settings screen is open. Links clicked while settings are open will be opened in the first matching browser automatically.
